blocking_findings.detail: GPL-2.0-with-FOSS-exception is incompatible with permissive-only project policy. CVE patched (8.0.30→8.0.31) but license replacement deferred to MAJOR_REVIEW issue #14.
blocking_findings.action_required: Replace with com.mysql:mysql-connector-j (permissive license) — see issue #14

warnings.detail: HIGH CVE with no fix available — not blocking per policy but requires monitoring
Post-Log4Shell medium CVE; fix available in 2.25.4 (minor version bump from 2.17.1)
Post-Log4Shell medium CVE; fix available in 2.25.3
Post-Log4Shell medium CVE; fix available in 2.25.4
MAJOR_REVIEW in progress — issue #13 opened; 24→32 jump requires human validation
GPL-2.0-with-classpath-exception — transitive dependency of guava; classpath exception makes it permissive-compatible in most cases

clean.typosquat: No typosquatted coordinates detected in SBOM
clean.untrusted_sources: Only Maven Central (https) in effective POM — HTTP mirror removed
clean.critical_cves_with_fix: Zero CRITICAL CVEs in Grype scan
clean.high_cves_with_fix: Zero HIGH CVEs with available fix in Grype scan

notes: BLOCK is due to pre-existing GPL-2.0 license violation on mysql-connector-java. CVE remediation for Log4Shell, jackson-databind, commons-io, commons-lang3, and mysql CVE-2022-21363 are all confirmed cleared by Grype. Typosquat and HTTP-mirror supply-chain issues resolved. Three medium CVEs in log4j-core 2.17.1 are addressable by bumping to 2.25.x (new separate fix recommended).
